(11 November 1752 – 14 March 1810) was a German [historian](/wiki/Historian "Historian") born in [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), Germany. He published works on national, [church](/wiki/Church_history "Church history") and [political history](/wiki/Political_history "Political history"). He was a member of the [Göttingen school of history](/wiki/G%C3%B6ttingen_school_of_history "Göttingen school of history").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

German historian (1752–1810)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article includes a [list of references](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources"), [related reading](/wiki/Wikipedia:Further_reading "Wikipedia:Further reading"), or [external links](/wiki/Wikipedia:External_links "Wikipedia:External links"), **but its sources remain unclear because it lacks [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources")**. Please help [improve](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Fact_and_Reference_Check "Wikipedia:WikiProject Fact and Reference Check") this article by [introducing](/wiki/Wikipedia:When_to_cite "Wikipedia:When to cite") more precise citations. *(June 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Ludwig Timotheus Spittler | |
| --- | --- |
| [Drawing of a person](/wiki/File:Ludwig_Timotheus_Spittler.jpg) Ludwig Timotheus Spittler | |
| Born | (1752-11-11)11 November 1752  [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), Germany |
| Died | 14 March 1810(1810-03-14) (aged 57) |

**Ludwig Timotheus Spittler** (11 November 1752 – 14 March 1810) was a German [historian](/wiki/Historian "Historian") born in [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), Germany. He published works on national, [church](/wiki/Church_history "Church history") and [political history](/wiki/Political_history "Political history"). He was a member of the [Göttingen school of history](/wiki/G%C3%B6ttingen_school_of_history "Göttingen school of history").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwig_Timotheus_Spittler&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

Spittler studied at [Tübingen](/wiki/University_of_T%C3%BCbingen "University of Tübingen"), and in 1778 became a full professor at the [University of Göttingen](/wiki/University_of_G%C3%B6ttingen "University of Göttingen"). At Göttingen he collaborated on several important projects with historians [August Ludwig von Schlözer](/wiki/August_Ludwig_von_Schl%C3%B6zer "August Ludwig von Schlözer") (1735–1809), [Johann Christoph Gatterer](/wiki/Johann_Christoph_Gatterer "Johann Christoph Gatterer") (1727–1799) and constitutional law teacher [Johann Stephan Pütter](/wiki/Johann_Stephan_P%C3%BCtter "Johann Stephan Pütter") (1725–1807). With [philosopher](/wiki/Philosopher "Philosopher") [Christoph Meiners](/wiki/Christoph_Meiners "Christoph Meiners") (1747–1810), he published the *Göttingische Historische Magazin* from January 1787 to August 1791.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwig_Timotheus_Spittler&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In 1786 he wrote *Geschichte des Fürstenthums Hannover seit der Reformation* ("History of the [Hanover](/wiki/Hanover "Hanover") Principality since the [Reformation](/wiki/Protestant_Reformation "Protestant Reformation")"), and in 1796, published *Geschichte der Dänischen Revolution im Jahr 1660*, a book in which he described how [Frederick III](/wiki/Frederick_III_of_Denmark "Frederick III of Denmark") introduced [political absolutism](/wiki/Absolutism_(European_history) "Absolutism (European history)") in [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark"). Another influential work of Spittler's was *Landesgeschichte in der Zeit der Deutschen Spataufklarung* ("The Country's History during the German Spataufklarung").

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwig_Timotheus_Spittler&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: References")]

* [Kant in the Classroom](http://www.manchester.edu/kant/Bio/FullBio/GattererJC.html) (biographical information on Johann Christoph Gatterer)
